channel.title: Suffolk's Law + Tech Feed
channel.description: The RSS Feed powering the Suffolk LIT Lab's Law + Tech News Bot.

item.title: The US government’s Anthropic models ban was never about an AI jailbreak
item.description:
The Trump administration's decision that forced Anthropic to pull its latest cybersecurity models could be reactionary, retaliatory, or both, but the message is clear: The AI industry isn't immune from U.S. government interference.

Source feed: https://techcrunch.com/feed/

item.title: Why You Should Choose Legal Ops Tools You Can Build On (Part 1)
item.description:
A strong development surface addresses problems that have not materialized yet. The post Why You Should Choose Legal Ops Tools You Can Build On (Part 1) appeared first on Above the Law.

Source feed: https://abovethelaw.com/feed/

item.title: Facebook’s new AI Mode search gets its info from public posts
item.description:
Your public Facebook posts could help inform AI-generated results in Meta's new AI Mode. When you search on Facebook, the "AI Mode" option will appear alongside the usual search modes like "People" and "Marketplace." It's one of several new AI features Meta is rolling out starting today, including photo presets that swap sports jerseys onto […]

Source feed: https://www.theverge.com/rss/index.xml

item.title: Xbox is closing down Hellblade creator Ninja Theory
item.description:
Xbox is closing down Ninja Theory, the studio behind the Hellblade series, a source tells The Verge. Staffers were told on a call on Monday about the closure, but they are hoping the studio will find a buyer. The closure comes as "several" Xbox studios at Microsoft, including Compulsion Games and Double Fine, are in […]

Source feed: https://www.theverge.com/rss/index.xml

item.title: COVID vaccines still protect against heart problems, large study finds
item.description:
Despite continued benefits, anti-vaccine rhetoric has driven down vaccination.

Source feed: http://feeds.arstechnica.com/arstechnica/index

item.title: Why You’re A Hypocrite
item.description:
What's a person to believe? The post Why You’re A Hypocrite appeared first on Above the Law.

Source feed: https://abovethelaw.com/feed/

item.title: UK to ban social media for kids under 16, may impose overnight curfews
item.description:
Critics say bans push kids to riskier alternatives and can be beaten with VPNs.

Source feed: http://feeds.arstechnica.com/arstechnica/index

item.title: Trump DOJ Friday News Dumps Its Approval Of The Job-Killing Paramount, Warner Bros Merger
item.description:
The Trump “Department of Justice’s” “antitrust division” dumped its unsurprising approval of the terrible Paramount Warner Brothers merger late on Friday in the hopes people wouldn’t notice it. As we’ve noted the $111 billion megadeal is a historically harmful mess. Backed by billions in Saudi and Chinese cash (raising all sorts of foreign media influence […]

Source feed: https://www.techdirt.com/feed/

item.title: Fox wants to take over your TV — and the tech inside it
item.description:
Fox is about to take over the TVs in more than 100 million homes worldwide. On Monday, Fox announced that it's acquiring Roku, the streaming middleman that serves as a portal for viewers to hop into services like Netflix, Disney Plus, Hulu, and more. The $22 billion deal may not change Roku's familiar purple interface, […]

Source feed: https://www.theverge.com/rss/index.xml

item.title: The ‘New’ Magic Circle Is Here, And U.S. Firms Dominate List
item.description:
A fresh take on London's legal elite puts American firms firmly in the spotlight. The post The ‘New’ Magic Circle Is Here, And U.S. Firms Dominate List appeared first on Above the Law.

Source feed: https://abovethelaw.com/feed/
